reused_existing_deps: pymupdf (fitz) -- already in agents/pyproject.toml, PDF write path new, read path pre-existing
PyYAML -- checking availability before committing to yaml vs json for topics manifest

risk_notes: Real live-Ollama generation calls are slow (~10-60s/doc on CPU per OllamaClient's own DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=120.0 comment) -- self-consistency run must use a small doc count, not the full ~30-50 topic target, to stay within reasonable time.

LLM output is non-deterministic in content even at temperature=0.0 across different runs/model versions -- test spec must assert on topic/keyword presence, not exact string equality, for the live smoke test; unit tests use a fake deterministic LLMClient for reliable assertions.

Must not introduce any OpenRouter/Gemini code path, import, or API-key reference anywhere in the new files -- verified by design (only llm.factory.create_llm_client(provider="ollama") is called, hardcoded).
data/ is not a Python package per agents/pyproject.toml; new script/test follow the existing data/load_bitext.py-style bare-module + sys.path convention, no build-system changes needed.
rollback_plan: New files only, zero modifications to existing modules; revertible by deleting the 3 new files with no other repo impact.
